title: Anthropic Considering Funding Offers at Over $900 Billion Value
snippet: Anthropic PBC has begun weighing a fresh funding round that would value the artificial intelligence developer at more than $900 billion, according to people familiar with the matter, potentially leapfrogging its longtim…
contentText: Anthropic PBC has begun weighing a fresh funding round that would value the artificial intelligence developer at more than $900 billion, according to people familiar with the matter, potentially leapfrogging its longtime rival OpenAI as the world’s most valuable AI startup.

title: What Latest Tech Earnings Signal for Apple, Nvidia

title: Meta Shares Plunge as AI Investments Raise Spending Outlook
snippet: Meta Platforms Inc. shares slid after the company raised its spending outlook for the year, reigniting fears that the historic levels of investment it’s making to build artificial intelligence models won’t pay off. The…
contentText: Meta Platforms Inc. shares slid after the company raised its spending outlook for the year, reigniting fears that the historic levels of investment it’s making to build artificial intelligence models won’t pay off. The social-media giant projected full-year capital expenditures between $125 billion and $145 billion, far exceeding analysts’ estimates and marking a roughly 7.4% increase from what the company had previously projected. The company is dealing with “higher component pricing” and additional data center costs, Chief Financial Officer Susan Li said in a statement. Bloomberg's Kurt Wagner joins to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The 10 Billion Startup Training AI To Do Your Job
snippet: San Francisco tech startup, Mercor, is hiring high-skilled workers for a massive undertaking: teaching AI how to do their jobs. On today’s Big Take podcast, reporter Tom Foster and host Sarah Holder break down how Merco…
contentText: San Francisco tech startup, Mercor, is hiring high-skilled workers for a massive undertaking: teaching AI how to do their jobs. On today’s Big Take podcast, reporter Tom Foster and host Sarah Holder break down how Mercor’s college-dropout founders built a $10 billion company aimed at automating the white-collar world. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Energy-Efficient Liquid Cooling for Advanced Semiconductor Packaging (KAIST)
snippet: A new technical paper, “Highly energy-efficient manifold microchannel for cooling electronics with a coefficient of performance over 100,000,” was published by researchers at KAIST. The study presents a CMOS-compatible…
contentText: A new technical paper, “Highly energy-efficient manifold microchannel for cooling electronics with a coefficient of performance over 100,000,” was published by researchers at KAIST. The study presents a CMOS-compatible manifold microchannel cooler that removes over 2,000 W/cm² using single-phase water at only 8 kPa pressure drop, achieving a record COP of 106,000—a significant improvement over prior state-of-the-art liquid-cooling technologies for advanced semiconductor packaging. The results point to a scalable thermal-management pathway for next-generation heterogeneous integration, 3D packaging, and AI/HPC chips, where thermal density is becoming a primary packaging bottleneck. Find the technical paper here . Published April 2026. Preprint download is available here . Lee,…

title: 80 Seconds of Big Tech Earnings To Set Market’s Fate | Bloomberg Tech 4/29/2026

contentText: Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow discuss expectations for big tech earnings as investors prepare for Microsoft, Meta, Google, and Amazon to report earnings after the market closes. And, Elon Musk continues testifying in his suit against OpenAI over the startup's pivot from a charity to a for-profit business. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Big Tech Faces Make or Break Moment | Open Interest 4/29/2026
snippet: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." 80 seconds that could set the market’s next move—Alphabet, Amazon, Meta and Microsoft all reporting after the bell. Starbucks CEO Bri…
contentText: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." 80 seconds that could set the market’s next move—Alphabet, Amazon, Meta and Microsoft all reporting after the bell. Starbucks CEO Brian Niccol joins Open Interest on fixing mobile orders and boosting consistency. A major Supreme Court ruling reshapes voting maps. Booking CEO Glenn Fogel on war-driven travel disruptions. Marsh CEO John Doyle on soaring insurance costs in the Hormuz crisis. And TD Cowen’s John Blackledge on whether Big Tech’s AI spending boom is reaching its limit. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: FCC to Review Disney-Owned TV Licenses Early
snippet: The Federal Communications Commission said it would do an early review of the licenses for eight Disney-owned TV stations. The move is tied to an investigation into the company’s diversity policy, but comes after a feud…
contentText: The Federal Communications Commission said it would do an early review of the licenses for eight Disney-owned TV stations. The move is tied to an investigation into the company’s diversity policy, but comes after a feud between President Donald Trump and ABC late-night host Jimmy Kimmel kicked back up. Bloomberg's Kelcee Griffis joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on “Bloomberg Tech.” (Source: Bloomberg)
